title: Usability fix: Make connections editable from the right click context menu
author: Brainiarc7
body_snippet:
<!-- Feature Request -->
<!-- If you file a feature request, please delete the bug section -->

As stated on the title: Make connection details editable from a right click context menu.
Having to go through a different panel to do the same thing over and over again is broken design.

iterations.description: Clear UI/UX enhancement request to allow editing connection properties directly from the right-click context menu, improving workflow efficiency

comments.author: kmscode
comments.snippet: Tentatively scheduled for 1.77... But I've got an idea or two that I might be able to squeeze into 1.76 if the rest of it goes smoothly...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage: Clear UI/UX enhancement request to allow editing connection properties directly from the right-click context menu, improving workflow efficiency
Approach: Add an 'Edit Connection' or 'Properties' menu item to the connection node right-click context menu that opens the connection's property editor, similar to double-click behavior
